messages.content:
1. Which one of the following is the best explanation of public relations?

a) Advertising b) Marketing

c) Business and government spinning negative events

d) Attempting to create a positive public image of an organization and its activities 2. An IT company is using 3% of its profit to finance the building of a new wing in a

local hospital in an effort to improve its corporate image. What is this an example of!

a) Advertising & promotion

b) Philanthropy & charity work

c) Sponsorship

d) External communication

e) Internal communication 1) Publicity

3. The CEO of your company is invited to speak on CNN about your company's success. He is going to use this opportunity to advertise your new product, a computer that is more than 30% faster than the best computer currently sold on the market. What is this

an

example of?

a) Advertising & promotion b) Philanthropy & charity work

c) Sponsorship

d) External communication

e) Internal communication

f) Publicity
f) Publicity

1. Which one of the following is the best explanation of public relations?

a) Advertising b) Marketing

c) Business and government spinning negative events

d) Attempting to create a positive public image of an organization and its activities
d) Attempting to create a positive public image of an organization and its activities

2. An IT company is using 3% of its profit to finance the building of a new wing in a

local hospital in an effort to improve its corporate image. What is this an example of!

a) Advertising & promotion

b) Philanthropy & charity work

c) Sponsorship

d) External communication

e) Internal communication 1) Publicity
b) Philanthropy & charity work

4. If a business is televising a commercial about a new product, what type of PR activity

is it using?

a) Advertising & promotion

b) Philanthropy & charity work

c) Sponsorship

d) External communication

c) Internal communication f) Publicity
a) Advertising & promotion

5. Identify which of the following is NOT an example of external communication:

a) Pamphlets which provide a potential customer with a 10% discount if he/she brings it b) A brochure with a detailed description of all new products of a retail company

to the store

c) Facebook post about the company's exclusive fall 50% discount on all items from the March collection d) A newsletter to employees that lists all of the benefits of working for the company
d) A newsletter to employees that lists all of the benefits of working for the company

6. What would be an example of a sponsorship? a) A local pizza place pays for a soccer team's uniforms and gets their company name on

the back of the t-shirt.

b) A bank donates money for a local women's breast cancer race. c) A toy company pays for Santa Claus to visit local orphanages: d) These are all examples of sponsorships.

7. If you receive a company memo from your supervisor, what's the best description

of this communication?

a) Informal vertical communication b) Informal horizontal communication

c) Formal vertical communication

d) Formal horizontal communication

8. You are a department head of the marketing department having a conference with the department heads from the financial department and production department about budgeting for a special project. What type of internal communication is being used in this situation?

a) Horizontal informal communication

b) Horizontal formal communication

c) Vertical informal communication

d) Vertical formal communication

9. Which of these best describes the job of journalist?

) Someone who finds and reports newsworthy stories. a

b) Someone who watches the news. c) Someone who promotes politicians and businesses.

10. Which of these headlines is NOT news?

a) US President to visit UK. b) Pupil drops pen during lesson.

c) Fukushima water release.

11. Your reports are done and they are about to be put on your newspaper website.

Which story should go first? a) The story your editor likes best.

b) The story that is most important to your audience.

c) The longest story.

12. Which statement about soft news is TRUE? a) Soft news can be considered entertainment.

b) Soft news is urgent.

c) Soft news affects most people's daily lives. d) Soft news covers politics.

6. d) These are all examples of sponsorships.
7. c) Formal vertical communication.
8. b) Horizontal formal communication.
9. a) Someone who finds and reports newsworthy stories.
10. b) Pupil drops pen during lesson.
11. b) The story that is most important to your audience.
12. a) Soft news can be considered entertainment.

13. Which statement about hard news is FALSE?

a) Hard news tends to be serious. b) Hard news is based on facts.

c) Hard news covers war updates.

d) Hard news covers topics like interior decorating.

14. Unlike hard news, soft news covers topics such as: a) Celebrity gossip or sports highlights

b) Political scandals

c) International affairs

d) Editorial pieces

15. What type of news story usually covers volunteer efforts and fundraising

activities?

a) hard news

b) soft news

16. When writing a news article using the inverted pyramid structure, the least important information should be placed:

a) In the second sentence

b) At the end of the first paragraph

c) In the middle of the article

d) At the end of the article

17. What is the golden rule for writing headlines?

a) Be as clever as possible b) Keep it short and bright

c) Never let the facts get in the way of a good story

18. The head teacher of a local primary school tells you that she's upset about a proposal to close her school. What headline would you choose for this story?

a) Head teacher announces school closure

b) Head teacher upset over school closure plan

c) Head teacher attacks council over school closure

19. Which of the five Ws would you use to find out information about the people

involved in the story or event?

a) What

b) Why

c) Who

d) When

13. d) Hard news covers topics like interior decorating.
14. a) Celebrity gossip or sports highlights
15. b) soft news
16. b) At the end of the first paragraph
17. b) Keep it short and bright
18. b) Head teacher upset over school closure plan
19. c) Who

21. What is another name for a news release?

a) press media b) bnews media

c) press release

d) media news

22. What is a news release?

a) a form of communication that is designed to attract consumers. b) a form of communication that is designed to attract media attention to important or

newsworthy events.

c) a form of communication that is designed to distract media attention away from true

events.

d) a form of communication that is designed to attract media attention for the sake of persuading others to give to charity.

23. How is a news release used?

a) Reporters use it when writing stories for media coverage.

b) It is sent to the patents office to gain a new product patent. c) It provides details for city planners when getting permits.

d) It is used for mass mailing to potential customers.

24. What is an important aspect of a news release?

a) The information is newsworthy.

b) The information is informative.

c) The information is time sensitive.

d) All of these answers are correct.

25. The first paragraph of your press release is the most important. What essential

elements should it include?

a) The who, what, when, where, and why of your information

b) How to contact you via telephone and email c) Where you can be found on social media

d) Only a photo of your product or service

26. Which of the following is an appropriate place to share your news release? a) With reporters at various publications

b) On your website

c) Via your social media channels

d) All answers are correct

21. c) press release
22. b) a form of communication that is designed to attract media attention to important or newsworthy events.
23. a) Reporters use it when writing stories for media coverage.
24. d) All of these answers are correct.
25. a) The who, what, when, where, and why of your information
26. d) All answers are correct

28. What is the attention grabber in a news release?

a) The release time b) The media contact

c) The headline

d) The dateline

29. Which of the following is NOT a journalistic element of an effective news release?

a) flashy images

b) timely releases

c) eye-catching headlines

d) the opening paragraph

30. The opening section of a business letter contains the

and

a) heading, recipient's address, salutation b) sender's address, recipient's address, greeting

c) date, recipient's address, salutation

d) sender's address, date, recipient's address

31. Which of the following is an appropriate closing phrase for a business letter?

a) Sincerely,

b) See you later!

c) Call me up! d) All of the answers are correct

32. What is a genre?

a) A way of thinking about communication and business.

b) A socially agreed upon and recognized form of communication developed over time to communicate more effectively and efficiently. c) A kind of avant-garde fiction.

33. In terms of its genre, every type of written communication usually has three

differences: its register, format and

a) page layout

b) title

c) tone

d) structure

28. c) The headline
29. a) flashy images
30. d) sender's address, date, recipient's address
31. a) Sincerely,
32. b) A socially agreed upon and recognized form of communication developed over time to communicate more effectively and efficiently.
33. c) tone

35. What is the difference between a written genre's format and structure? a) Format refers to a document's set of expected topics and structure refers to the level of formality or language used.

on a page.

expected topics.

b) Format refers to a document's layout and structure refers to the way a document looks c) Format refers to a document's layout and structure refers to the document's set of

36. The tone of business letters should be.

complain about something.

a) polite and formal

b) antagonistic and threatening

c) short and to the point

d) gender neutral and short

37. Can the purpose for memos be persuasive? b) No

a) Yes

38. Formal tone should ALWAYS be used for Memo writing

a) True b) False

39. The purpose of a memo should be in the

a) Middle paragraph. b) Closing paragraph.

c) Opening paragraph.

40. Business email writers should always write a clear subject line which

) politely addresses the reader a

b) illustrates the conclusion of the email c) describes the purpose of the email

35. b) Format refers to a document's layout and structure refers to the way a document looks on a page.
36. a) polite and formal
37. a) Yes
38. b) False
39. c) Opening paragraph.
40. c) describes the purpose of the email
